scenario.title: Claim for battery wear after software update-induced accelerated degradation
scenario.description: Customer submits a battery capacity claim shortly after a major iOS update reporting accelerated battery degradation that appears hardware-related but coincides with the software update timeframe.

service_fee.notes: Battery service under Section 3.1 is free if capacity degradation exceeds 50%, but interplay with software update raises ambiguity.

policy_references.snippet: Apple will service battery defects if capacity has depleted 50% or more from original specifications.
Technical support includes issues with operating system and software affecting hardware performance.
Damage caused by operating the iPhone outside permitted or intended uses is not covered.
Customer must provide information including symptoms, software version, and actions before issue occurred.

scenario.reasoning: The claim is ambiguous because accelerated battery degradation could be due to a hardware defect covered under Section 3.1 or a software-induced condition potentially excluded by Section 4.2(ii)(c). Technical support per Section 3.3 may assist diagnosis. Customer’s detailed info on software updates and usage is required per Section 7(iii). No ADH service fee applies as this is battery health degradation. Judgment is needed whether the battery failure is a hardware defect or software-related, impacting coverage.

persona.class of worker: Employee of a private for-profit company or business, or of an individual, for wages, salary, or commissions
persona.detailed job description: Serves customers, takes orders, and ensures customer satisfaction
persona.education: 1 or more years of college credit, no degree
persona.household type: Female householder, no spouse/partner present, only nonrelatives present
persona.mannerisms: Tends to be spontaneous and impulsive
persona.marital status: Never married or under 15 years old
persona.personal time: Spends free time exploring new places or trying out new activities

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario description:
Customer submits a battery capacity claim shortly after a major iOS update reporting accelerated battery degradation that appears hardware-related but coincides with the software update timeframe.

Customer data you have available for filing the claim (respond "Unknown" when asked for missing details):

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hi there! I recently updated my iPhone 14 Pro Max to the latest iOS, and since then, my battery seems to be draining way faster than usual. I think it might be a hardware issue because the degradation looks pretty accelerated. I’d like to file a battery capacity claim under my AppleCare plan, please.
messages.raw_text: Hi there! I recently updated my iPhone 14 Pro Max to the latest iOS, and since then, my battery seems to be draining way faster than usual. I think it might be a hardware issue because the degradation looks pretty accelerated. I’d like to file a battery capacity claim under my AppleCare plan, please.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: I’m here to assist with that. To start, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or your iPhone 14 Pro Max's serial number? This will help us validate your coverage.
messages.raw_text: I’m here to assist with that. To start, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or your iPhone 14 Pro Max's serial number? This will help us validate your coverage.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure thing! My Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-9857362012, and the serial number is F4N7W8C5R2ZQ. Thanks for helping me out with this.
messages.raw_text: Sure thing! My Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-9857362012, and the serial number is F4N7W8C5R2ZQ. Thanks for helping me out with this.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing that information. Could you please describe the battery issue in more detail? For example, how quickly is the battery draining, and have you noticed any other related symptoms? Also, have you performed any troubleshooting steps?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing that information. Could you please describe the battery issue in more detail? For example, how quickly is the battery draining, and have you noticed any other related symptoms? Also, have you performed any troubleshooting steps?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Yeah, since the update, the battery drops from full to like 20% within a few hours even when I'm just using it for basic stuff like calls or checking messages. Normally, I’d get a whole day out of it with a lot more use. No overheating or weird crashes yet, but the quick drain is new and feels hardware-related to me, especially since it started right after the iOS update. I haven’t done much troubleshooting except restarting it a couple times. Thought it’s better to get professional help first so I don’t accidentally mess anything up.
messages.raw_text: Yeah, since the update, the battery drops from full to like 20% within a few hours even when I'm just using it for basic stuff like calls or checking messages. Normally, I’d get a whole day out of it with a lot more use. No overheating or weird crashes yet, but the quick drain is new and feels hardware-related to me, especially since it started right after the iOS update. I haven’t done much troubleshooting except restarting it a couple times. Thought it’s better to get professional help first so I don’t accidentally mess anything up.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thanks for the details—that helps a lot. To proceed, can you confirm the current maximum battery capacity percentage shown in your iPhone’s Battery Health settings? We need to check if it has dropped 50% or more from the original capacity. Also, could you let me know the exact iOS version currently installed on your iPhone?
messages.raw_text: Thanks for the details—that helps a lot. To proceed, can you confirm the current maximum battery capacity percentage shown in your iPhone’s Battery Health settings? We need to check if it has dropped 50% or more from the original capacity. Also, could you let me know the exact iOS version currently installed on your iPhone?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure, the battery health shows 65% maximum capacity right now, which is definitely a big drop from when I got it. And I'm running iOS 17.2—just got the update a few days ago. Seems like the drop started happening immediately after I updated.
messages.raw_text: Sure, the battery health shows 65% maximum capacity right now, which is definitely a big drop from when I got it. And I'm running iOS 17.2—just got the update a few days ago. Seems like the drop started happening immediately after I updated.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for confirming those details. With that, I have all the information needed to file your battery capacity claim under your AppleCare plan. Is there anything else I can help you with today before I proceed with processing your claim?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for confirming those details. With that, I have all the information needed to file your battery capacity claim under your AppleCare plan. Is there anything else I can help you with today before I proceed with processing your claim?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, that’s all for now. Thanks so much for helping me out. Please go ahead and file the claim—I really appreciate it!
messages.raw_text: No, that’s all for now. Thanks so much for helping me out. Please go ahead and file the claim—I really appreciate it!

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Your claim will be processed, and you will be notified as soon as there is an update. If you need any further assistance in the future, feel free to reach out. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: Your claim will be processed, and you will be notified as soon as there is an update. If you need any further assistance in the future, feel free to reach out. Have a great day! <END>
